title: onosma microcarpa var. stapfii
intro: Onosma microcarpa var. stapfii, also known as a species of flowering plant in the family Apocynaceae, is a type of Onosma, a genus of plants commonly referred to as "globe mallows" due to their globe-shaped seed pods. Traditionally, the plant has been used in various forms for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal activities. The health benefits of Onosma microcarpa var. stapfii include its potential to alleviate symptoms of fever, cough, and respiratory issues, as well as its use in treating wounds and skin conditions. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, with its roots and leaves being used separately or in combination for medicinal purposes.
